raw_text:
Noted. If Phase 16 is truly locked (46/46, CI lints in place, E2E hits real NATS+PG), then Phase 17 should pivot from “foundations” to “operational truth at scale.”

Here’s the priority stack I’d run.

Phase 17 Priorities
P0 Track A — Projection Integrity and Replay Governance

Objective: make “read models” provably correct, rebuildable, and observable.

Projection cursoring: last_event_id / stream_position per projection table

Projection lag metrics: projection_lag_ms, projection_backlog

Rebuild protocol: deterministic rebuild (blue/green swap), no partial readers

Fallback policy: explicit HTTP fallback behavior (budgets + circuit breaker)

Replay certification: “replay → same digest” tests for at least 2 core projections

Why now: you’ve hardened emits; now you must harden the read side or you’ll ship a system that’s correct but not reliably queryable.

P0 Track B — Audit Spine by Mutation Class

Objective: turn mutation_class into enforceable audit behavior.

Audit event emission rules per mutation class (strict_immutable vs compensating_required vs mutable_with_audit)

Field-level diffs for mutable_with_audit changes

Actor + cause capture (who/what/why), tied to envelope metadata

Tamper-evident audit chain (append-only audit log, no updates)

E2E audit tests: “every covered mutation emits exactly one audit record”

Why now: you declared policy; Phase 17 is where policy becomes proof.

P0 Track C — Tenant Provisioning and Isolation Readiness

Objective: make onboarding and ops repeatable for 10 → 1,000 tenants.

Tenant provisioning runbook + automation (DB init, migrations, seed data)

Schema/version registry per module

Tenant lifecycle: create → activate → suspend → deprovision

Migration runner that can execute N DBs deterministically (no manual yak shaving)

Load simulation: 100–500 tenants with billing cycles (use your deterministic harness pattern)

Why now: once you sell, tenant ops becomes your bottleneck if not automated.

P1 Add-ons (only after P0 is green)

SLO dashboards tied to the new metrics + alerts (UNKNOWN aging, invariant fail rate, projection lag)

Hardening security surfaces (authn/authz boundaries, service-to-service trust, secrets handling)

DR drill automation (restore + replay rebuild + oracle pass)

My Recommendation: Phase 17 = “Truth at Scale”

If you do only one thing next: Projection cursoring + deterministic rebuild + replay certification.
That’s what separates “production-ready foundations” from “production-grade system.”

If you want, I’ll output the Phase 17 beads JSON in the same bead format you’re using (single-concern, integrated verification, explicit invariants).
